Turing Pattern Sphere Explorer

Reaction-diffusion on a sphere — Schnakenberg model (cf. Miyazawa 2020)

Pattern type
Dark spots ←→ Labyrinths ←→ Light spots on dark
Pattern strength (r)
Larger = bolder, higher-contrast patterns
How it works
A short-range activator promotes its own production. A long-range inhibitor suppresses it from farther away. The balance between them creates spatial patterns.
• Dark = high activator (pigmented)
• Light = low activator (unpigmented)
The pattern type slider controls the balance: spots ↔ labyrinths ↔ inverse spots. Same equations, different parameters.
EEB 187 — Ecology and Evolution of Color | Model: Schnakenberg (1979); cf. Miyazawa (2020) Sci Adv 6:eabb9107
